agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Architecture for a real-time ML delivery time estimation service on the checkout critical path. The design separates the system into four bounded contexts: Feature Engineering, Model Training Pipeline, Model Serving, and Monitoring/Rollback. The serving layer uses pre-loaded models with a lightweight inference path to hit <100ms P99. A shadow-scoring pattern enables safe weekly model promotion with automatic rollback. The architecture is sized for a 4-person team (2 ML, 2 platform) by favoring managed services and keeping custom code minimal.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Feature Store","responsibility":"Computes, stores, and serves features for both training (batch) and inference (online). Maintains point-in-time correctness for training features and low-latency access for serving features. Canonical feature definitions live here to prevent training/serving skew.","boundaries":"Owns feature computation logic and storage. Does NOT own raw data ingestion or model logic. Online store is a Redis-backed key-value layer; offline store is Parquet on object storage."},{"name":"Training Pipeline","responsibility":"Orchestrates weekly model retraining: pulls labeled delivery data, joins with offline feature store, trains a gradient-boosted model (XGBoost/LightGBM), evaluates against hold-out set, and publishes a versioned model artifact to the Model Registry.","boundaries":"Owns training DAG, hyperparameter config, and evaluation metrics. Does NOT decide whether to promote a model — that decision belongs to the Promotion Gate."},{"name":"Model Registry","responsibility":"Stores versioned, immutable model artifacts with metadata (training date, metrics, feature schema hash, status: candidate/active/rollback). Supports at minimum 2 active versions: current production and previous for rollback.","boundaries":"Owns artifact storage and version metadata. Does NOT serve predictions or decide promotions."},{"name":"Prediction Service","responsibility":"Stateless gRPC/HTTP service that loads the active model into memory at startup and on promotion events. Accepts order features, fetches any real-time features from the online feature store, runs inference, and returns predicted delivery time in minutes. Must meet <100ms P99 SLA.","boundaries":"Owns inference execution and request validation. Does NOT own feature computation logic (delegates to Feature Store) or model training."},{"name":"Promotion Gate","responsibility":"Evaluates candidate models against the current production model using shadow scoring and statistical tests (e.g., MAE comparison on live traffic sample). Decides promote/reject/rollback. Emits model-switched domain events.","boundaries":"Owns promotion criteria and rollback triggers. Operates between the Training Pipeline and the Prediction Service."},{"name":"Monitoring & Observability","responsibility":"Tracks prediction latency (P50/P95/P99), model accuracy (predicted vs actual delivery time when ground truth arrives), feature drift, and data quality. Triggers rollback alerts when accuracy degrades beyond threshold.","boundaries":"Read-only observation layer. Does NOT modify models or features directly — triggers rollback via Promotion Gate."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Prediction Service","to":"Feature Store (Online)","contract":"GET /features/{order_id} → { weight, distance, zone, time_of_day, weather, courier_load } — latency budget: <10ms","coupling_type":"runtime dependency (synchronous, low-latency)"},{"from":"Training Pipeline","to":"Feature Store (Offline)","contract":"Batch read of historical features joined with delivery outcomes as Parquet dataset","coupling_type":"batch dependency (asynchronous)"},{"from":"Training Pipeline","to":"Model Registry","contract":"PUT /models/{version} with artifact blob + metadata (metrics, feature_schema_hash)","coupling_type":"artifact dependency (asynchronous)"},{"from":"Promotion Gate","to":"Model Registry","contract":"PATCH /models/{version}/status → active|rolled_back; GET /models?status=active","coupling_type":"control plane (asynchronous)"},{"from":"Promotion Gate","to":"Prediction Service","contract":"Event: ModelPromoted { version, artifact_uri } — service hot-reloads model without restart","coupling_type":"event-driven (pub/sub)"},{"from":"Monitoring & Observability","to":"Promotion Gate","contract":"Alert: ModelDegradation { current_version, mae_delta, threshold } triggers automatic rollback evaluation","coupling_type":"event-driven (asynchronous)"},{"from":"Checkout Service (external)","to":"Prediction Service","contract":"POST /predict { weight, distance_km, zone_id, time_of_day, weather_code, courier_load } → { estimated_minutes, confidence_interval, model_version }","coupling_type":"synchronous HTTP/gRPC — on critical checkout path"}],"data_flow":"1. Checkout service sends order features to Prediction Service. 2. Prediction Service enriches with real-time features from Online Feature Store (courier load, weather — pre-computed and cached). 3. Inference runs in-process on pre-loaded model (<100ms total). 4. Response returned to checkout. --- Offline: Raw delivery data lands in data lake → Feature Store computes offline features → Training Pipeline joins features + labels → trains model → publishes to Model Registry → Promotion Gate shadow-scores → promotes or rejects → Prediction Service hot-reloads on promotion event.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Feature Store (Online + Offline)","rationale":"Eliminates training/serving skew — the #1 cause of ML production failures. Single source of truth for feature definitions used in both training and serving."},{"pattern":"Model Registry with Immutable Artifacts","rationale":"Enables instant rollback by keeping the previous model version always available. Immutability guarantees reproducibility."},{"pattern":"Shadow Scoring / Champion-Challenger","rationale":"New candidate model scores live traffic in shadow mode alongside the production model. Statistical comparison before promotion prevents shipping degraded models."},{"pattern":"Hot Model Reload via Events","rationale":"Prediction service subscribes to model promotion events and swaps the in-memory model without restarts, maintaining the 100ms P99 SLA during transitions with zero downtime."},{"pattern":"Pre-computation + Caching for Real-time Features","rationale":"Weather and courier load are pre-computed on short intervals (30s–1min) and cached in Redis. This moves expensive I/O out of the critical inference path, keeping P99 under budget."},{"pattern":"Circuit Breaker with Fallback","rationale":"If the ML model or feature store is unavailable, fall back to a simple heuristic (zone-based lookup table). Checkout must never be blocked by ML infrastructure."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Feature Engineering","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["FeatureDefinition","FeatureSet","DataSource"]},{"name":"Model Lifecycle","classification":"core","aggregates":["ModelArtifact","TrainingRun","PromotionDecision"]},{"name":"Prediction Serving","classification":"core","aggregates":["PredictionRequest","PredictionResponse","ServingModel"]},{"name":"Monitoring","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["AccuracyMetric","LatencyMetric","DriftAlert"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Feature Engineering","downstream":"Model Lifecycle","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Training Pipeline specifies what features it needs; Feature Engineering computes and serves them"},{"upstream":"Model Lifecycle","downstream":"Prediction Serving","relationship":"Open Host Service — Model Registry exposes versioned artifacts via standard API; Prediction Serving consumes without coupling to training internals"},{"upstream":"Prediction Serving","downstream":"Monitoring","relationship":"Conformist — Monitoring observes prediction logs as-is; no translation needed"},{"upstream":"Monitoring","downstream":"Model Lifecycle","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Monitoring triggers rollback signals that Promotion Gate consumes"}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"In-process model inference (embedded XGBoost/LightGBM) rather than a separate model serving framework (TensorFlow Serving, Triton)","gains":"Sub-10ms inference latency, simpler deployment, fewer moving parts for a 4-person team, easy to debug","sacrifices":"Harder to serve deep learning models later, requires application restart or hot-reload logic for model updates","alternatives_considered":["TensorFlow Serving — adds operational overhead and network hop, overkill for tabular gradient-boosted models","Triton Inference Server — powerful but complex, justified only at higher scale or with GPU models"]},{"decision":"Gradient-boosted trees (XGBoost/LightGBM) as initial model choice","gains":"Fast inference (<5ms for tabular data), interpretable, excellent for structured/tabular features, easy to train weekly on modest compute","sacrifices":"May underperform deep learning on very large datasets with complex feature interactions","alternatives_considered":["Neural network — higher latency, harder to explain, requires GPU for fast inference, premature for the current feature set"]},{"decision":"Redis-backed online feature store rather than real-time feature computation per request","gains":"Predictable <10ms feature fetch, decouples feature freshness from serving latency","sacrifices":"Features can be slightly stale (30s–1min), requires a separate feature materialization job","alternatives_considered":["Compute features on-the-fly — simpler architecture but unpredictable latency for weather/courier load lookups that hit external APIs"]},{"decision":"Shadow scoring for model promotion rather than offline evaluation only","gains":"Catches real-world distribution shifts that offline hold-out sets miss, high-confidence promotion decisions","sacrifices":"Requires running two models simultaneously for a scoring window (hours), slightly higher compute cost during evaluation","alternatives_considered":["A/B testing with live traffic split — more statistically rigorous but risks exposing users to a degraded model, and adds routing complexity"]},{"decision":"Event-driven hot-reload rather than blue-green deployment for model swaps","gains":"Zero-downtime model updates, no need for duplicate infrastructure, simpler for small team","sacrifices":"Requires careful implementation of thread-safe model swap, brief inconsistency window during reload","alternatives_considered":["Blue-green deployment — cleaner isolation but doubles infrastructure cost and operational burden for a 4-person team"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Model algorithm","evolution_path":"The serving layer is model-agnostic — it loads a predict() interface. Can swap XGBoost for LightGBM, CatBoost, or even an ONNX-exported neural network without changing the serving infrastructure."},{"area":"Feature set","evolution_path":"Adding new features requires: (1) add feature definition to Feature Store, (2) update training pipeline to include it, (3) retrain. No serving code changes needed if features flow through the store."},{"area":"Retraining cadence","evolution_path":"Weekly retraining is a cron schedule parameter. Can increase to daily or trigger on data drift detection without architectural changes."},{"area":"Serving scale","evolution_path":"Prediction Service is stateless — horizontal scaling is trivial. Can move from single-node to auto-scaled cluster by adding a load balancer."},{"area":"Model complexity","evolution_path":"If deep learning becomes necessary, the Model Registry and Promotion Gate are model-agnostic. Only the Prediction Service inference layer needs adaptation (e.g., switch to ONNX Runtime or add GPU nodes)."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Feature Store as single source of truth","reason":"All feature logic MUST go through the Feature Store. Allowing ad-hoc feature computation in the serving path will immediately create training/serving skew — the most dangerous ML production bug."},{"area":"Model Registry immutability","reason":"Model artifacts must never be mutated once published. Rollback depends on the previous version being identical to what was originally deployed."},{"area":"Promotion Gate as mandatory checkpoint","reason":"No model reaches production without passing through the Promotion Gate. Bypassing this (e.g., manually deploying a model) defeats the safety guarantees."},{"area":"100ms P99 latency budget","reason":"This is a checkout-path SLA. Any architectural change must preserve this budget: ~10ms feature fetch + ~5ms inference + ~15ms network/serialization overhead = ~30ms typical, leaving 70ms headroom for P99 tail."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Training/serving skew — features computed differently in training vs serving leads to silent accuracy degradation","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Feature Store enforces single feature definitions used by both pipelines. Feature schema hash is stored with each model version; serving rejects models whose schema doesn't match."},{"risk":"Model degradation goes undetected — new model is worse but metrics aren't monitored closely","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Promotion Gate requires shadow scoring pass before promotion. Monitoring continuously compares predicted vs actual delivery times (ground truth available within hours). Automatic rollback trigger on MAE exceeding threshold."},{"risk":"Feature store latency spike breaks P99 SLA","severity":"high","mitigation":"Circuit breaker on feature store calls with fallback to cached features (slightly stale) or heuristic fallback. Redis read timeout set to 15ms — if exceeded, use last-known feature values."},{"risk":"Stale features cause prediction inaccuracy — courier load or weather changes faster than materialization interval","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Tune materialization interval (start at 30s). For courier load, the checkout request itself can carry the most recent value from the order management system, reducing dependency on cached data."},{"risk":"Small team cannot maintain the full ML platform","severity":"high","mitigation":"Use managed services where possible: managed Redis (ElastiCache/Memorystore), managed orchestration (Airflow/Cloud Composer), object storage for model registry. Minimize custom infrastructure. Total custom code should be: prediction service (~500 LOC), training pipeline (~800 LOC), feature definitions (~300 LOC), promotion gate (~400 LOC)."},{"risk":"Hot model reload causes brief serving inconsistency","severity":"low","mitigation":"Use atomic pointer swap pattern: load new model in background, swap reference atomically. Requests in-flight complete with old model; new requests use new model. No locking required with copy-on-write semantics."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase 1 (Weeks 1-3): Platform engineers build the Prediction Service with a hardcoded heuristic fallback model and Redis-based feature serving. ML engineers set up the Feature Store definitions and training pipeline producing XGBoost models. Phase 2 (Weeks 4-5): Integrate — Prediction Service loads models from Model Registry, feature store serves online features. Build the Promotion Gate with basic MAE comparison. Phase 3 (Week 6): Add monitoring dashboards, automatic rollback triggers, and shadow scoring. This phasing lets the checkout integration go live early with the heuristic fallback while the ML pipeline matures behind it.","files_to_create_or_modify":["services/prediction-service/main.py — FastAPI/gRPC server, model loading, inference endpoint","services/prediction-service/model_loader.py — Hot-reload logic, atomic model swap, model registry client","services/prediction-service/fallback.py — Zone-based heuristic lookup table for circuit breaker fallback","services/prediction-service/features.py — Online feature store client with Redis, timeout, and caching","pipelines/training/train.py — Weekly training DAG: data pull, feature join, XGBoost train, evaluate, publish","pipelines/training/evaluate.py — Hold-out evaluation, metric computation, threshold checks","pipelines/features/definitions.py — Canonical feature definitions shared by training and serving","pipelines/features/materialize.py — Batch and streaming feature materialization jobs","services/promotion-gate/gate.py — Shadow scoring comparison, statistical tests, promote/reject/rollback logic","services/promotion-gate/rollback.py — Rollback trigger handler, model version management","infra/model-registry/registry.py — Model artifact storage, versioning, metadata API (or use MLflow)","monitoring/dashboards/ — Grafana dashboards for latency, accuracy, drift","monitoring/alerts.py — Alert rules for MAE degradation, latency SLA breach, feature drift"],"patterns_to_follow":"Use the Predict interface abstraction (model.predict(features) → minutes) so the serving layer is model-agnostic. All features flow through FeatureStore — never compute features inline in the serving path. Model artifacts are immutable and versioned with monotonically increasing version numbers. Use structured logging with model_version, prediction_id, and latency_ms on every prediction for monitoring joins. Keep the serving path dependency-light: no Pandas in serving, use raw numpy arrays for inference.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["DO NOT use Pandas in the serving path — it adds 20-50ms overhead for DataFrame construction. Use numpy arrays directly for XGBoost/LightGBM inference.","DO NOT skip the Feature Store and compute features ad-hoc in serving code — training/serving skew is the #1 ML production killer.","DO NOT deploy models without shadow scoring — offline metrics do not reliably predict production performance due to distribution shift.","DO NOT use a single model version pointer without keeping the previous version warm — rollback must be instant (seconds, not minutes).","DO NOT block checkout on ML infrastructure failure — always have a heuristic fallback that returns a reasonable estimate.","DO NOT store model artifacts in the application container image — this couples deployment to model release and prevents independent model updates.","DO NOT use floating-point equality for feature schema validation — use schema hashes to detect training/serving feature mismatches."]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":85,"high_confidence_areas":["Serving architecture — in-process gradient-boosted model inference is well-proven for <100ms latency on tabular data","Feature Store pattern — industry-standard approach to prevent training/serving skew","Rollback mechanism — immutable versioned artifacts with instant swap is straightforward","Team sizing — the phased approach and managed-service-heavy design is appropriate for 4 engineers"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact feature materialization latency for courier load — depends on upstream data source characteristics not specified","Shadow scoring statistical test choice — depends on traffic volume and acceptable Type II error rate, needs ML team input","Infrastructure cost — managed service costs depend on cloud provider and traffic volume not specified"],"assumptions":["Traffic volume is moderate (hundreds to low thousands of predictions per second) — enough to warrant Redis but not extreme scale","Cloud-hosted infrastructure with access to managed Redis, managed Airflow, and object storage","Delivery ground truth (actual delivery time) is available within hours for monitoring and weekly for retraining","The 6 input features listed are representative — the architecture supports adding more without structural changes","XGBoost/LightGBM model size is <100MB and fits comfortably in memory on a single serving node"]}}

The serving layer uses pre-loaded models with a lightweight inference path to hit <100ms P99. A shadow-scoring pattern enables safe weekly model promotion with automatic rollback. No locking required with copy-on-write semantics."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase 1 (Weeks 1-3): Platform engineers build the Prediction Service with a hardcoded heuristic fallback model and Redis-based feature serving. ML engineers set up the Feature Store definitions and training pipeline producing XGBoost models. Phase 2 (Weeks 4-5): Integrate — Prediction Service loads models from Model Registry, feature store serves online features. Build the Promotion Gate with basic MAE comparison. Phase 3 (Week 6): Add monitoring dashboards, automatic rollback triggers, and shadow scoring. This phasing lets the checkout integration go live early with the heuristic fallback while the ML pipeline matures behind it.","files_to_create_or_modify":["services/prediction-service/main.py — FastAPI/gRPC server, model loading, inference endpoint","services/prediction-service/model_loader.py — Hot-reload logic, atomic model swap, model registry client","services/prediction-service/fallback.py — Zone-based heuristic lookup table for circuit breaker fallback","services/prediction-service/features.py — Online feature store client with Redis, timeout, and caching","pipelines/training/train.py — Weekly training DAG: data pull, feature join, XGBoost train, evaluate, publish","pipelines/training/evaluate.py — Hold-out evaluation, metric computation, threshold checks","pipelines/features/definitions.py — Canonical feature definitions shared by training and serving","pipelines/features/materialize.py — Batch and streaming feature materialization jobs","services/promotion-gate/gate.py — Shadow scoring comparison, statistical tests, promote/reject/rollback logic","services/promotion-gate/rollback.py — Rollback trigger handler, model version management","infra/model-registry/registry.py — Model artifact storage, versioning, metadata API (or use MLflow)","monitoring/dashboards/ — Grafana dashboards for latency, accuracy, drift","monitoring/alerts.py — Alert rules for MAE degradation, latency SLA breach, feature drift"],"patterns_to_follow":"Use the Predict interface abstraction (model.predict(features) → minutes) so the serving layer is model-agnostic. All features flow through FeatureStore — never compute features inline in the serving path. Model artifacts are immutable and versioned with monotonically increasing version numbers. Use structured logging with model_version, prediction_id, and latency_ms on every prediction for monitoring joins. Keep the serving path dependency-light: no Pandas in serving, use raw numpy arrays for inference.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["DO NOT use Pandas in the serving path — it adds 20-50ms overhead for DataFrame construction. Use numpy arrays directly for XGBoost/LightGBM inference.","DO NOT skip the Feature Store and compute features ad-hoc in serving code — training/serving skew is the #1 ML production killer.","DO NOT deploy models without shadow scoring — offline metrics do not reliably predict production performance due to distribution shift.","DO NOT use a single model version pointer without keeping the previous version warm — rollback must be instant (seconds, not minutes).","DO NOT block checkout on ML infrastructure failure — always have a heuristic fallback that returns a reasonable estimate.","DO NOT store model artifacts in the application container image — this couples deployment to model release and prevents independent model updates.","DO NOT use floating-point equality for feature schema validation — use schema hashes to detect training/serving feature mismatches."]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":85,"high_confidence_areas":["Serving architecture — in-process gradient-boosted model inference is well-proven for <100ms latency on tabular data","Feature Store pattern — industry-standard approach to prevent training/serving skew","Rollback mechanism — immutable versioned artifacts with instant swap is straightforward","Team sizing — the phased approach and managed-service-heavy design is appropriate for 4 engineers"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact feature materialization latency for courier load — depends on upstream data source characteristics not specified","Shadow scoring statistical test choice — depends on traffic volume and acceptable Type II error rate, needs ML team input","Infrastructure cost — managed service costs depend on cloud provider and traffic volume not specified"],"assumptions":["Traffic volume is moderate (hundreds to low thousands of predictions per second) — enough to warrant Redis but not extreme scale","Cloud-hosted infrastructure with access to managed Redis, managed Airflow, and object storage","Delivery ground truth (actual delivery time) is available within hours for monitoring and weekly for retraining","The 6 input features listed are representative — the architecture supports adding more without structural changes","XGBoost/LightGBM model size is <100MB and fits comfortably in memory on a single serving node"]}}

trace.result: {"executive_summary":"Architecture for a real-time ML delivery time estimation service on the checkout critical path. The design separates the system into four bounded contexts: Feature Engineering, Model Training Pipeline, Model Serving, and Monitoring/Rollback. The serving layer uses pre-loaded models with a lightweight inference path to hit <100ms P99. A shadow-scoring pattern enables safe weekly model promotion with automatic rollback. The architecture is sized for a 4-person team (2 ML, 2 platform) by favoring managed services and keeping custom code minimal.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Feature Store","responsibility":"Computes, stores, and serves features for both training (batch) and inference (online). Maintains point-in-time correctness for training features and low-latency access for serving features. Canonical feature definitions live here to prevent training/serving skew.","boundaries":"Owns feature computation logic and storage. Does NOT own raw data ingestion or model logic. Online store is a Redis-backed key-value layer; offline store is Parquet on object storage."},{"name":"Training Pipeline","responsibility":"Orchestrates weekly model retraining: pulls labeled delivery data, joins with offline feature store, trains a gradient-boosted model (XGBoost/LightGBM), evaluates against hold-out set, and publishes a versioned model artifact to the Model Registry.","boundaries":"Owns training DAG, hyperparameter config, and evaluation metrics. Does NOT decide whether to promote a model — that decision belongs to the Promotion Gate."},{"name":"Model Registry","responsibility":"Stores versioned, immutable model artifacts with metadata (training date, metrics, feature schema hash, status: candidate/active/rollback). Supports at minimum 2 active versions: current production and previous for rollback.","boundaries":"Owns artifact storage and version metadata. Does NOT serve predictions or decide promotions."},{"name":"Prediction Service","responsibility":"Stateless gRPC/HTTP service that loads the active model into memory at startup and on promotion events. Accepts order features, fetches any real-time features from the online feature store, runs inference, and returns predicted delivery time in minutes. Must meet <100ms P99 SLA.","boundaries":"Owns inference execution and request validation. Does NOT own feature computation logic (delegates to Feature Store) or model training."},{"name":"Promotion Gate","responsibility":"Evaluates candidate models against the current production model using shadow scoring and statistical tests (e.g., MAE comparison on live traffic sample). Decides promote/reject/rollback. Emits model-switched domain events.","boundaries":"Owns promotion criteria and rollback triggers. Operates between the Training Pipeline and the Prediction Service."},{"name":"Monitoring & Observability","responsibility":"Tracks prediction latency (P50/P95/P99), model accuracy (predicted vs actual delivery time when ground truth arrives), feature drift, and data quality. Triggers rollback alerts when accuracy degrades beyond threshold.","boundaries":"Read-only observation layer. Does NOT modify models or features directly — triggers rollback via Promotion Gate."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Prediction Service","to":"Feature Store (Online)","contract":"GET /features/{order_id} → { weight, distance, zone, time_of_day, weather, courier_load } — latency budget: <10ms","coupling_type":"runtime dependency (synchronous, low-latency)"},{"from":"Training Pipeline","to":"Feature Store (Offline)","contract":"Batch read of historical features joined with delivery outcomes as Parquet dataset","coupling_type":"batch dependency (asynchronous)"},{"from":"Training Pipeline","to":"Model Registry","contract":"PUT /models/{version} with artifact blob + metadata (metrics, feature_schema_hash)","coupling_type":"artifact dependency (asynchronous)"},{"from":"Promotion Gate","to":"Model Registry","contract":"PATCH /models/{version}/status → active|rolled_back; GET /models?status=active","coupling_type":"control plane (asynchronous)"},{"from":"Promotion Gate","to":"Prediction Service","contract":"Event: ModelPromoted { version, artifact_uri } — service hot-reloads model without restart","coupling_type":"event-driven (pub/sub)"},{"from":"Monitoring & Observability","to":"Promotion Gate","contract":"Alert: ModelDegradation { current_version, mae_delta, threshold } triggers automatic rollback evaluation","coupling_type":"event-driven (asynchronous)"},{"from":"Checkout Service (external)","to":"Prediction Service","contract":"POST /predict { weight, distance_km, zone_id, time_of_day, weather_code, courier_load } → { estimated_minutes, confidence_interval, model_version }","coupling_type":"synchronous HTTP/gRPC — on critical checkout path"}],"data_flow":"1. Checkout service sends order features to Prediction Service. 2. Prediction Service enriches with real-time features from Online Feature Store (courier load, weather — pre-computed and cached). 3. Inference runs in-process on pre-loaded model (<100ms total). 4. Response returned to checkout. --- Offline: Raw delivery data lands in data lake → Feature Store computes offline features → Training Pipeline joins features + labels → trains model → publishes to Model Registry → Promotion Gate shadow-scores → promotes or rejects → Prediction Service hot-reloads on promotion event.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Feature Store (Online + Offline)","rationale":"Eliminates training/serving skew — the #1 cause of ML production failures. Single source of truth for feature definitions used in both training and serving."},{"pattern":"Model Registry with Immutable Artifacts","rationale":"Enables instant rollback by keeping the previous model version always available. Immutability guarantees reproducibility."},{"pattern":"Shadow Scoring / Champion-Challenger","rationale":"New candidate model scores live traffic in shadow mode alongside the production model. Statistical comparison before promotion prevents shipping degraded models."},{"pattern":"Hot Model Reload via Events","rationale":"Prediction service subscribes to model promotion events and swaps the in-memory model without restarts, maintaining the 100ms P99 SLA during transitions with zero downtime."},{"pattern":"Pre-computation + Caching for Real-time Features","rationale":"Weather and courier load are pre-computed on short intervals (30s–1min) and cached in Redis. This moves expensive I/O out of the critical inference path, keeping P99 under budget."},{"pattern":"Circuit Breaker with Fallback","rationale":"If the ML model or feature store is unavailable, fall back to a simple heuristic (zone-based lookup table). Checkout must never be blocked by ML infrastructure."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Feature Engineering","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["FeatureDefinition","FeatureSet","DataSource"]},{"name":"Model Lifecycle","classification":"core","aggregates":["ModelArtifact","TrainingRun","PromotionDecision"]},{"name":"Prediction Serving","classification":"core","aggregates":["PredictionRequest","PredictionResponse","ServingModel"]},{"name":"Monitoring","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["AccuracyMetric","LatencyMetric","DriftAlert"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Feature Engineering","downstream":"Model Lifecycle","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Training Pipeline specifies what features it needs; Feature Engineering computes and serves them"},{"upstream":"Model Lifecycle","downstream":"Prediction Serving","relationship":"Open Host Service — Model Registry exposes versioned artifacts via standard API; Prediction Serving consumes without coupling to training internals"},{"upstream":"Prediction Serving","downstream":"Monitoring","relationship":"Conformist — Monitoring observes prediction logs as-is; no translation needed"},{"upstream":"Monitoring","downstream":"Model Lifecycle","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Monitoring triggers rollback signals that Promotion Gate consumes"}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"In-process model inference (embedded XGBoost/LightGBM) rather than a separate model serving framework (TensorFlow Serving, Triton)","gains":"Sub-10ms inference latency, simpler deployment, fewer moving parts for a 4-person team, easy to debug","sacrifices":"Harder to serve deep learning models later, requires application restart or hot-reload logic for model updates","alternatives_considered":["TensorFlow Serving — adds operational overhead and network hop, overkill for tabular gradient-boosted models","Triton Inference Server — powerful but complex, justified only at higher scale or with GPU models"]},{"decision":"Gradient-boosted trees (XGBoost/LightGBM) as initial model choice","gains":"Fast inference (<5ms for tabular data), interpretable, excellent for structured/tabular features, easy to train weekly on modest compute","sacrifices":"May underperform deep learning on very large datasets with complex feature interactions","alternatives_considered":["Neural network — higher latency, harder to explain, requires GPU for fast inference, premature for the current feature set"]},{"decision":"Redis-backed online feature store rather than real-time feature computation per request","gains":"Predictable <10ms feature fetch, decouples feature freshness from serving latency","sacrifices":"Features can be slightly stale (30s–1min), requires a separate feature materialization job","alternatives_considered":["Compute features on-the-fly — simpler architecture but unpredictable latency for weather/courier load lookups that hit external APIs"]},{"decision":"Shadow scoring for model promotion rather than offline evaluation only","gains":"Catches real-world distribution shifts that offline hold-out sets miss, high-confidence promotion decisions","sacrifices":"Requires running two models simultaneously for a scoring window (hours), slightly higher compute cost during evaluation","alternatives_considered":["A/B testing with live traffic split — more statistically rigorous but risks exposing users to a degraded model, and adds routing complexity"]},{"decision":"Event-driven hot-reload rather than blue-green deployment for model swaps","gains":"Zero-downtime model updates, no need for duplicate infrastructure, simpler for small team","sacrifices":"Requires careful implementation of thread-safe model swap, brief inconsistency window during reload","alternatives_considered":["Blue-green deployment — cleaner isolation but doubles infrastructure cost and operational burden for a 4-person team"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Model algorithm","evolution_path":"The serving layer is model-agnostic — it loads a predict() interface. Can swap XGBoost for LightGBM, CatBoost, or even an ONNX-exported neural network without changing the serving infrastructure."},{"area":"Feature set","evolution_path":"Adding new features requires: (1) add feature definition to Feature Store, (2) update training pipeline to include it, (3) retrain. No serving code changes needed if features flow through the store."},{"area":"Retraining cadence","evolution_path":"Weekly retraining is a cron schedule parameter. Can increase to daily or trigger on data drift detection without architectural changes."},{"area":"Serving scale","evolution_path":"Prediction Service is stateless — horizontal scaling is trivial. Can move from single-node to auto-scaled cluster by adding a load balancer."},{"area":"Model complexity","evolution_path":"If deep learning becomes necessary, the Model Registry and Promotion Gate are model-agnostic. Only the Prediction Service inference layer needs adaptation (e.g., switch to ONNX Runtime or add GPU nodes)."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Feature Store as single source of truth","reason":"All feature logic MUST go through the Feature Store. Allowing ad-hoc feature computation in the serving path will immediately create training/serving skew — the most dangerous ML production bug."},{"area":"Model Registry immutability","reason":"Model artifacts must never be mutated once published. Rollback depends on the previous version being identical to what was originally deployed."},{"area":"Promotion Gate as mandatory checkpoint","reason":"No model reaches production without passing through the Promotion Gate. Bypassing this (e.g., manually deploying a model) defeats the safety guarantees."},{"area":"100ms P99 latency budget","reason":"This is a checkout-path SLA. Any architectural change must preserve this budget: ~10ms feature fetch + ~5ms inference + ~15ms network/serialization overhead = ~30ms typical, leaving 70ms headroom for P99 tail."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Training/serving skew — features computed differently in training vs serving leads to silent accuracy degradation","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Feature Store enforces single feature definitions used by both pipelines. Feature schema hash is stored with each model version; serving rejects models whose schema doesn't match."},{"risk":"Model degradation goes undetected — new model is worse but metrics aren't monitored closely","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Promotion Gate requires shadow scoring pass before promotion. Monitoring continuously compares predicted vs actual delivery times (ground truth available within hours). Automatic rollback trigger on MAE exceeding threshold."},{"risk":"Feature store latency spike breaks P99 SLA","severity":"high","mitigation":"Circuit breaker on feature store calls with fallback to cached features (slightly stale) or heuristic fallback. Redis read timeout set to 15ms — if exceeded, use last-known feature values."},{"risk":"Stale features cause prediction inaccuracy — courier load or weather changes faster than materialization interval","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Tune materialization interval (start at 30s). For courier load, the checkout request itself can carry the most recent value from the order management system, reducing dependency on cached data."},{"risk":"Small team cannot maintain the full ML platform","severity":"high","mitigation":"Use managed services where possible: managed Redis (ElastiCache/Memorystore), managed orchestration (Airflow/Cloud Composer), object storage for model registry. Minimize custom infrastructure. Total custom code should be: prediction service (~500 LOC), training pipeline (~800 LOC), feature definitions (~300 LOC), promotion gate (~400 LOC)."},{"risk":"Hot model reload causes brief serving inconsistency","severity":"low","mitigation":"Use atomic pointer swap pattern: load new model in background, swap reference atomically. Requests in-flight complete with old model; new requests use new model. No locking required with copy-on-write semantics."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase 1 (Weeks 1-3): Platform engineers build the Prediction Service with a hardcoded heuristic fallback model and Redis-based feature serving. ML engineers set up the Feature Store definitions and training pipeline producing XGBoost models. Phase 2 (Weeks 4-5): Integrate — Prediction Service loads models from Model Registry, feature store serves online features. Build the Promotion Gate with basic MAE comparison. Phase 3 (Week 6): Add monitoring dashboards, automatic rollback triggers, and shadow scoring. This phasing lets the checkout integration go live early with the heuristic fallback while the ML pipeline matures behind it.","files_to_create_or_modify":["services/prediction-service/main.py — FastAPI/gRPC server, model loading, inference endpoint","services/prediction-service/model_loader.py — Hot-reload logic, atomic model swap, model registry client","services/prediction-service/fallback.py — Zone-based heuristic lookup table for circuit breaker fallback","services/prediction-service/features.py — Online feature store client with Redis, timeout, and caching","pipelines/training/train.py — Weekly training DAG: data pull, feature join, XGBoost train, evaluate, publish","pipelines/training/evaluate.py — Hold-out evaluation, metric computation, threshold checks","pipelines/features/definitions.py — Canonical feature definitions shared by training and serving","pipelines/features/materialize.py — Batch and streaming feature materialization jobs","services/promotion-gate/gate.py — Shadow scoring comparison, statistical tests, promote/reject/rollback logic","services/promotion-gate/rollback.py — Rollback trigger handler, model version management","infra/model-registry/registry.py — Model artifact storage, versioning, metadata API (or use MLflow)","monitoring/dashboards/ — Grafana dashboards for latency, accuracy, drift","monitoring/alerts.py — Alert rules for MAE degradation, latency SLA breach, feature drift"],"patterns_to_follow":"Use the Predict interface abstraction (model.predict(features) → minutes) so the serving layer is model-agnostic. All features flow through FeatureStore — never compute features inline in the serving path. Model artifacts are immutable and versioned with monotonically increasing version numbers. Use structured logging with model_version, prediction_id, and latency_ms on every prediction for monitoring joins. Keep the serving path dependency-light: no Pandas in serving, use raw numpy arrays for inference.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["DO NOT use Pandas in the serving path — it adds 20-50ms overhead for DataFrame construction. Use numpy arrays directly for XGBoost/LightGBM inference.","DO NOT skip the Feature Store and compute features ad-hoc in serving code — training/serving skew is the #1 ML production killer.","DO NOT deploy models without shadow scoring — offline metrics do not reliably predict production performance due to distribution shift.","DO NOT use a single model version pointer without keeping the previous version warm — rollback must be instant (seconds, not minutes).","DO NOT block checkout on ML infrastructure failure — always have a heuristic fallback that returns a reasonable estimate.","DO NOT store model artifacts in the application container image — this couples deployment to model release and prevents independent model updates.","DO NOT use floating-point equality for feature schema validation — use schema hashes to detect training/serving feature mismatches."]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":85,"high_confidence_areas":["Serving architecture — in-process gradient-boosted model inference is well-proven for <100ms latency on tabular data","Feature Store pattern — industry-standard approach to prevent training/serving skew","Rollback mechanism — immutable versioned artifacts with instant swap is straightforward","Team sizing — the phased approach and managed-service-heavy design is appropriate for 4 engineers"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact feature materialization latency for courier load — depends on upstream data source characteristics not specified","Shadow scoring statistical test choice — depends on traffic volume and acceptable Type II error rate, needs ML team input","Infrastructure cost — managed service costs depend on cloud provider and traffic volume not specified"],"assumptions":["Traffic volume is moderate (hundreds to low thousands of predictions per second) — enough to warrant Redis but not extreme scale","Cloud-hosted infrastructure with access to managed Redis, managed Airflow, and object storage","Delivery ground truth (actual delivery time) is available within hours for monitoring and weekly for retraining","The 6 input features listed are representative — the architecture supports adding more without structural changes","XGBoost/LightGBM model size is <100MB and fits comfortably in memory on a single serving node"]}}
